info.title: AxisFi API 接口文档
info.description: 认证需同时提供 API Key 和 Secret

post.description: Executes a fund transfer from the source wallet to a designated recipient. This covers both internal account movements and external payouts. Requires a secure transaction PIN or multi-factor authentication where applicable.
Updates an existing recipient's profile. Since this uses the PATCH method, only the fields provided in the request body will be modified. The ID in the path must match the recipient's record.
Removes a recipient from the organization's address book. Please note that recipients with active or pending transaction history may be restricted from deletion to maintain financial audit trails.
Registers a new recipient (beneficiary) in the address book. Requires valid financial destination details such as bank account numbers (IBAN/SWIFT), routing information, or digital wallet addresses.
Retrieves real-time currency exchange rates or formal quotes for specified currency pairs. Rates are sourced from global market data and include applicable spreads.
Uploads a single document or image to the server. Supports specific categories such as invoices and contracts. Returns a unique file ID and the accessible URL upon successful storage.

fileType.description:
Document Type Code. Valid values vary based on the specific business scenario:

### 1. Customer Corporate Documents
* `Business Registration`: Official business license.
* `Article of Association`: Corporate constitutional documents.

### 2. Customer Legal Representative ID
* `Front / Main page`: Front side of the ID card or Passport bio-data page.
* `Back page`: Back side of the ID card.

### 3. Transfer Documents Or Recipient Documents
* `Invoice`: Billing document for payment request.
* `Contract`: Supporting service/goods contract.

> **Note**: Ensure the uploaded `documentType` matches the specific business context (Customer,Recipient,Transfer).

post.description: Creates a new deposit request to fund a specified wallet. This supports both fiat (bank transfer) and crypto deposits. Returns a transaction reference ID for tracking.
Modifies an existing customer's information. As this is a PATCH request, only the fields included in the request body will be updated. The path ID must match the customer record to be modified.

Removes a customer record from the system. This action may be restricted if the customer has active wallets, pending transactions, or outstanding balances to ensure data integrity and audit compliance.
Registers a new customer entity in the system. Requires mandatory identity information, contact details, and entity classification (Individual or Corporate).

get.description: Returns account information for fiat and crypto wallets.

Retrieves a comprehensive list of financial transactions. Supports advanced filtering by date range, transaction status (e.g., Pending, Completed, Failed), and currency pairs for audit and reconciliation purposes.
Retrieves the full lifecycle data of a transaction. Includes precise timestamps, fee structures, currency conversion rates (if applicable), and detailed status history for reconciliation.
Retrieves the complete profile of a specific recipient (beneficiary) by ID. Includes bank account information, routing details, crypto addresses, and current verification status.
Retrieves the binary content of a file by its unique identifier. The response is streamed directly with the appropriate Content-Type and Content-Disposition headers for browser downloading.
Retrieves comprehensive profile information for a specific customer by ID, including entity type, contact details, and current verification status.
Returns a comprehensive list of all balances across fiat and crypto wallets. Includes available funds, pending amounts, and currency-specific details.

remaining_seconds.description: Remaining seconds until expiration (zero or negative if expired)

account_usage_product_reference.pattern: ^(Financial Services|Payment Processing & Card Services|Retail & Trading|Business & Professional Services)$

X-API-Key.description: Includes an API key in the HTTP headers to authenticate the client.
X-API-Key.name: X-API-Key

X-API-Secret.description: Includes an API secret in the HTTP headers to authenticate the client.
X-API-Secret.name: X-API-Secret
